---
title: "How to run notebooks?"
---

## Start here (no coding setup needed)

In this course, we use **Jupyter notebooks**: interactive pages where text, code, and results appear together.

You will see the notebooks on **GitHub**. Think of GitHub as a public online folder for course code and files.

For beginners, the easiest method is to open notebooks in **Google Colab** (runs in your browser, no installation required).

## Notebook order for this course

1. [01 Colab Intro](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/01_colab_intro.ipynb)
2. [02 Python Brush up](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/02_Python_Brush_up.ipynb)
3. [03 Vectorizing Texts](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/03_VectorizingTexts.ipynb)
4. [04 Vector Space Model (Egyptian)](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/04_Vector_Space_Model_Egyptian.ipynb)
5. [05 Vector Space Model](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/05_Vector_Space_Model.ipynb)
6. [06 PMI (A. Sahala)](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/06_PMI_ASahala.ipynb)
7. [09 Vectors to Networks](https://github.com/DigitalPasts/alpcourse/blob/main/notebooks/09_Vectors_to_Networks.ipynb)

## How to run a notebook from GitHub (recommended)

1. Open one notebook link above.
2. On the GitHub page, click **Open in Colab**.  
	(If you do not see this button, copy the GitHub URL and open: <https://colab.research.google.com/> → **GitHub** tab → paste URL.)
3. In Colab, click **Copy to Drive** so you can edit your own copy.
4. Run cells top-to-bottom with **Shift+Enter**.
5. If asked, click **Connect** (top-right) to start the runtime.

## Important habits

- Always run cells in order from top to bottom.
- Save your work in your own Google Drive copy.
- If a cell fails, restart runtime and run all cells again.
